findings.title: Global Administrator Count (4)
findings.description: Found 4 Global Administrator assignments. Microsoft recommends 2-4 Global Admins maximum. Best practice: use cloud-only accounts, PIM eligible assignments, and break-glass procedures.
findings.recommendation: Reduce Global Admin count to 2-4. Use PIM for eligible (not permanent) assignments. Ensure at least 2 cloud-only break-glass accounts exist.

findings.title: High-Risk OAuth Consent Grants (12)
findings.description: 12 OAuth grants include high-risk scopes that allow applications to read/write sensitive data (mail, files, directory).

findings.recommendation: Review and revoke unnecessary OAuth grants. Implement admin consent workflow.

findings.title: No Conditional Access Policies Found
findings.description: No Conditional Access policies were retrieved. This could mean: (1) No policies are configured - critical security gap, or (2) Insufficient permissions to read CA policies (Policy.Read.All required).
findings.recommendation: Configure Conditional Access policies for: MFA enforcement, device compliance, trusted locations, risky sign-in blocking, and legacy auth blocking.

findings.title: Token Replay Indicators (2 events)
findings.description: Detected 2 instances where the same user accessed the same application from different IP addresses within 5 minutes. This may indicate stolen tokens being replayed by an attacker.
affected_objects: sshook@forgepointcap.com (Microsoft Graph Command Line Tools): 208.250.98.39 → 34.29.175.97 in 228s
findings.recommendation: Review if users were using VPN or mobile networks. If not, revoke sessions and investigate for token theft.

findings.title: High-Risk Application Permissions (28 grants)
findings.description: Found 28 high-risk Graph API permissions granted to 7 applications. These permissions allow reading/writing mail, files, or directory data.
affected_objects: SkyKick Cloud Manager - Exchange Online: RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory
findings.recommendation: Review if each application requires these permissions. Remove unnecessary grants. Prefer delegated over application permissions.

findings.title: Email Domain Security (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) for 1 domain(s)
findings.description: Analyzed email authentication records for 1 domain(s). Missing or weak SPF/DKIM/DMARC enables email spoofing and phishing attacks impersonating your organization.
affected_objects: forgepointcap.com: SPF: ✓ | DKIM: ✓ | DMARC: ✓ p=none — DMARC p=none provides monitoring only, no enforcement
findings.recommendation: Implement SPF with -all (hard fail), DKIM signing via M365/Google, and DMARC p=reject. This is critical given the Mail.Send permissions in this tenant.

findings.title: Legacy Authentication Usage (3 events, 0.4%)
findings.description: 3 sign-ins used legacy authentication protocols which bypass MFA. 1 unique users affected.
findings.recommendation: Block legacy authentication via Conditional Access policy.

findings.title: High IP Address Diversity (1 users)
findings.description: 1 users authenticated from 10+ distinct IP addresses during the analysis period. May indicate credential sharing, VPN rotation, or compromised credentials used by multiple actors.
findings.recommendation: Review if IP diversity is expected (mobile users, VPN). Excessive diversity may indicate credential compromise.

findings.title: Applications with Mail.Send Permission (3 apps)
findings.description: 3 applications have Mail.Send permission, allowing them to send email on behalf of users. If compromised, these apps could be used for phishing or BEC attacks.
findings.recommendation: Verify each app requires Mail.Send. Consider using send-on-behalf-of with specific mailboxes instead of tenant-wide permission.

findings.title: Inactive Users Without MFA (7)
findings.description: 7 enabled users have no MFA and no recent sign-in activity in the analysis period. These may be provisioned but unused accounts.
findings.recommendation: Review if these accounts are needed. Consider disabling unused accounts or require MFA enrollment before first use.

findings.title: Guest Accounts Without Local MFA (14)
findings.description: 14 external guest accounts have no MFA registered in your tenant. Guest MFA is typically enforced by their home tenant's policies, not your tenant's MFA registration.
findings.recommendation: Configure Conditional Access to require MFA for guest access to sensitive apps. MFA challenge will be handled by guest's home tenant or your tenant as fallback.

findings.title: External Guest Users (15 accounts from 4 domains)
findings.description: Found 15 guest/external user accounts from 4 external domains. Guest accounts can access shared resources and should be governed via access reviews.
findings.recommendation: Implement guest access reviews. Configure B2B collaboration settings. Consider blocking guest access to sensitive apps via Conditional Access.

findings.title: Off-Hours Authentication Activity (139 events)
findings.description: 139 successful sign-ins occurred outside business hours (10pm-6am or weekends) from 10 users with 5+ events. While some may be legitimate, review for unauthorized access.
findings.recommendation: Verify off-hours access aligns with user job requirements. Consider time-based Conditional Access for sensitive roles.

findings.title: SharePoint Access from Multiple Locations (3 users)
findings.description: 3 users accessed SharePoint from 3+ different geographic locations. While this may be normal for traveling users, verify if access patterns are expected.
affected_objects: chiggins@forgepointcap.com: 3 locations - Boston, US, Roanoke, US, Salem, US
jlau@forgepointcap.com: 3 locations - Belmont, US, Brentwood (Contra Costa), US, San Francisco, US
nbassi@forgepointcap.com: 3 locations - Atherton, US, East Palo Alto, US, San Mateo, US
findings.recommendation: Review if geographic diversity is expected for these users.

findings.title: Room/Service Accounts Without MFA (6)
findings.description: 6 accounts appear to be room resources or service accounts based on naming patterns. These often don't require MFA if secured via Conditional Access (block external) or used non-interactively.
findings.recommendation: Verify these accounts are secured via CA policies blocking external access, or are non-interactive with strong passwords. Consider converting to resource mailboxes where appropriate.

findings.title: Critical Role Assignment Summary (15 roles in use)
findings.description: Summary of assignments to critical administrative roles. Review each role to ensure assignments follow least-privilege principles.
affected_objects: Exchange Administrator (3): llee.admin@forgepointcap.com, admin.sshook@forgepointcap.com, SkyKick Cloud Manager - Exchange Online
findings.recommendation: Document business justification for each privileged assignment. Consider using PIM for just-in-time activation.

findings.title: Failed Sign-in Attempts (125 events)
findings.description: 125 failed sign-in attempts from 14 unique accounts. Review for potential brute-force or credential stuffing attacks.
findings.recommendation: Investigate repeated failures. Consider smart lockout policies.
